result:
All line numbers confirmed. Here's the full picture.

## (a) Kconfig option enabling e1000e

**`drivers/net/ethernet/intel/Kconfig:58`**
```
config E1000E
	tristate "Intel(R) PRO/1000 PCI-Express Gigabit Ethernet support"
	depends on PCI && (!SPARC32 || BROKEN)
	depends on PTP_1588_CLOCK_OPTIONAL
	select CRC32
```
(There's no separate `Kconfig` file inside the `e1000e/` driver directory itself ŌĆö the driver's own option is declared at the vendor level.)

## (b) Makefile rule(s) compiling e1000e objects

**`drivers/net/ethernet/intel/Makefile:8`** ŌĆö descends into the driver dir:
```
obj-$(CONFIG_E1000E) += e1000e/
```
**`drivers/net/ethernet/intel/e1000e/Makefile:11`** ŌĆö builds the module:
```
obj-$(CONFIG_E1000E) += e1000e.o
```
**`drivers/net/ethernet/intel/e1000e/Makefile:13-15`** ŌĆö object list composing `e1000e.o`:
```
e1000e-objs := 82571.o ich8lan.o 80003es2lan.o \
	       mac.o manage.o nvm.o phy.o \
	       param.o ethtool.o netdev.o ptp.o
```

## (c) All build-system gating layers, top ŌåÆ bottom

| Layer | File:line | Gate |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | `drivers/net/Kconfig:6,27` | `menuconfig NETDEVICES` (bool, default y) ŌĆ” `if NETDEVICES` opens at line 27, `endif # NETDEVICES` closes at line 647 |
| 2 | `drivers/net/Kconfig:490` | `source "drivers/net/ethernet/Kconfig"` (inside the `if NETDEVICES` block) |
| 3 | `drivers/net/Makefile:52` | `obj-$(CONFIG_ETHERNET) += ethernet/` |
| 4 | `drivers/net/ethernet/Kconfig:6,13` | `menuconfig ETHERNET` (bool "Ethernet driver support", depends on NET, default y) ŌĆ” `if ETHERNET` opens at line 13, `endif # ETHERNET` closes at line 196 |
| 5 | `drivers/net/ethernet/Kconfig:86` | `source "drivers/net/ethernet/intel/Kconfig"` (inside `if ETHERNET`) |
| 6 | `drivers/net/ethernet/Makefile:50` | `obj-$(CONFIG_NET_VENDOR_INTEL) += intel/` |
| 7 | `drivers/net/ethernet/intel/Kconfig:6,17` | `config NET_VENDOR_INTEL` (bool "Intel devices", default y) ŌĆ” `if NET_VENDOR_INTEL` opens at line 17 |
| 8 | `drivers/net/ethernet/intel/Kconfig:58` | `config E1000E` itself (the driver's own option ŌĆö see (a)) |
| 9 | `drivers/net/ethernet/intel/Makefile:8` | `obj-$(CONFIG_E1000E) += e1000e/` ŌĆö descends into the driver directory |
| 10 | `drivers/net/ethernet/intel/e1000e/Makefile:11,13-15` | `obj-$(CONFIG_E1000E) += e1000e.o` + `e1000e-objs :=` object list ŌĆö see (b) |

So to actually build e1000e, the config chain that must resolve true/module is: `NETDEVICES` ŌåÆ `ETHERNET` ŌåÆ `NET_VENDOR_INTEL` ŌåÆ `E1000E` (plus `E1000E`'s own `depends on PCI && (!SPARC32 || BROKEN)` and `depends on PTP_1588_CLOCK_OPTIONAL`).

**Note on repo scope**: this checkout is a pruned subset of the kernel tree ŌĆö there is no top-level `Kconfig` or `drivers/Kconfig` file present (only `net/Kconfig`, `drivers/net/...`, etc. exist), so I couldn't trace the very top of the chain (`drivers/Kconfig` ŌåÆ `source "drivers/net/Kconfig"`, and the top `Makefile`'s `core-y += drivers/`). Within the files actually present, `drivers/net/Kconfig` and `drivers/net/Makefile` are the highest-level entry points, and the chain above from there down to the driver is complete and grep-verified (not just ccodegraph-derived, since Kconfig/Makefile files aren't C source and thus aren't indexed in the graph).
